memoryArtifacts.headline: tree_Moores_Ancestry
memoryArtifacts.description: This document was compiled by Orville Sullivan L6L2-JJB who is a grandson of Carrie Moore and Delos Sullivan, I was given a paper copy and decided to scan it in and create the searchable text form of the document so others may see and use it. I  tagged a number of the people found in the document to make it easier for others to locate.

memoryArtifacts.headline: tree_Sullivan_Family_Sheet
memoryArtifacts.description:
This family sheet was found in papers of Mildred Evelyn Sullivan LNKH-8PV a granddaugher of Delos.

It was created by her brother Orville Oscar  Sullivan L6L2-JJB who had done a great deal of searching for ancestors. 

Many of the children of Delos appear to have had children.  None were entered at this time (7 July 2020) as I do not know their status

Nte that for Fay William the birth is Sept 21 not 2

zh.lifeSummary: 當Carrie Moore於1871年5月30日出生在美國威斯康辛州克勞福德普萊利 度 希時，她的父親Cornelious S Moore33歲，母親Mary Agnes Borland34歲。她於1894年7月21日與Delos Horace Sullivan在美國愛阿華州法也特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她於1920年住在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特巴度拉 堂史普且於1930年住在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特托德 堂史普。她去世於1930年4月15日在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特耐維斯號享年58歲，埋葬在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特帕克 拉皮茲格林伍德 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Moore
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English more ‘moor, marsh, fen’ (Old English mōr), hence a topographic name for someone who lived in such a place, or a habitational name from any of various places called with this word, as for example Moore in Cheshire or More in Shropshire.
English (of Norman origin): ethnic name from Old French more ‘Moor’, either someone from North Africa or, more often, a nickname for someone thought to resemble a Moor. Compare Morrell and Moreau .

English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus), originally denoting either ‘Moor’ or someone with a swarthy complexion (compare Morrell , Morrin , Morris , and sense 2 above). There was a 6th-century Christian saint of this name.

variants.name: Moor
meanings: English, Irish, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Moore  .
German: variant of   Mohr  .

Dutch (also De Moor): nickname for a man of swarthy complexion, from Middle Dutch moor ‘Moor’ (from Latin maurus), or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a Moor. Compare De Moor .

variants.name: More
meanings: 1  Scottish, English:  see  Moore  .

variants.name: Morrin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Morin, a diminutive of More (Old French More, Maur, Latin Maurus); see Moore 2 and 3.
English: perhaps sometimes from the Middle English personal name Morwin (Old English Mōrwine, the first element of which is uncertain and the second wine ‘friend’).

variants.name: Mauro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Sal, Domenic, Giuseppe, Antonio, Carmine, Carmelo, Domenico, Gaetano, Nino, Pasquale.

Italian (mainly southern): from the personal name Mauro, derived from the Latin personal and ethnic name Maurus, borne by various early Christian saints and meaning ‘Moor, North African’. Compare Moore and Moro .

variants.name: Moresco
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Primo, Gino, Luciano, Riccardo.
Italian:
(Morésco): from a medieval personal name or a nickname for someone from North Africa or a swarthy or dark-haired man, from medieval Latin Mauriscus ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2).

variants.name: Morey
meanings: Irish (Limerick and Cork): variant of   Moore   4.
English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Mory, a pet form of Amauri (see Amery ).
English: perhaps also a variant of   Moore   1–3.

variants.name: Morrell
meanings: English (northern): from the medieval personal name Morel, a diminutive of Middle English or Old French More. Compare Moore .
Americanized form of French   Morel  . Compare   Morell   3.

variants.name: Moores
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): variant of Moore , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: McMoore
meanings: Irish: variant of Moore 4, in which Mac- ‘son of’ has been prefixed in modern times to give the name (Ó Mórdha ‘descendant of Mórdha’) an unmistakably Gaelic identity.

variants.name: Moro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Mario, Alberto, Jorge, Lino, Luis, Luiz, Manuel, Ramiro, Adolfo, Aida.  Italian Angelo, Pino, Pio, Remo, Antonio, Ciro, Cosmo, Elio, Enrico, Geno, Giancarlo, Gino.
Spanish: nickname or ethnic name from moro ‘Moor’, from Latin Maurus ‘Moor, North African’.
Italian: nickname from moro ‘Moor’ (see Moore 2, compare 1 above), also ‘black-haired man’.

nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of Caroline or occasionally of other girls' names beginning with the syllable Car-, now often bestowed as a name in its own right.

historicEvents.title: The First National Park
historicEvents.description: Yellowstone National Park was given the title of the first national park by the U.S. Congress and signed into law by President Ulysses S. Grant. It is also believed to be the first national park in the world.
Historical Boundaries: 1873: Faulk, Dakota Territory, United States 1889: Faulk, South Dakota, United States

historicEvents.title: The Sherman Antitrust Act
historicEvents.description: This Act tried to prevent the raising of prices by restricting trade. The purpose of the Act was to preserve a competitive marketplace to protect consumers from abuse.
Historical Boundaries: 1899: Hubbard, Minnesota, United States

historicEvents.title: So Much Farm Land
historicEvents.description: A law that funded many irrigation and agricultural projects in the western states.

historicEvents.title: The End of Capital Punishment
historicEvents.description: Between the state's entry into the Union and 1906, 27 people were executed by hanging in Minnesota. Public opinion soon shifted against the death penalty in the state and was formally abolished in 1911. Since its abolishment, there have been 23 attempts to reinstate the death penalty, but none of these bills passed the state legislature.
U.S. intervenes in World War I, rejects membership of League of Nations.

historicEvents.title: Camp Dodge
historicEvents.description: Original construction of the Camp Dodge began in 1907 and was originally planned to provide a place for the National Guard units to train. In 1917, it was handed over to national authorities and expanded to become the regional training center for World War I forces. The Camp was named after Brigadier General Grenville M. Dodge, who organized Iowa's first National Guard unit. When the war ended, the camp was downsized and turned back over to the state until the start of World War II. Today, Camp Dodge has served only as a Guard and Reserve installation.

en.lifeSummary: When Carrie Moore was born on 30 May 1871, in Prairie du Chien, Crawford, Wisconsin, United States, her father, Cornelious S Moore, was 33 and her mother, Mary Agnes Borland, was 34. She married Delos Horace Sullivan on 21 July 1894, in Fayette, Iowa, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 3 daughters. She lived in Badoura Township, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States in 1920 and Todd Township, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States in 1930. She died on 15 April 1930, in Nevis, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States, at the age of 58, and was buried in Greenwood Cemetery, Park Rapids, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States.

ja.lifeSummary: Carrie Mooreが1871年5月30日にアメリカ合衆国ウィスコンシン州クラウフォードプレアイリー ダ チンで生まれた時，父Cornelious S Mooreは33歳，母Mary Agnes Borlandは34歳であった。1894年7月21日にアメリカ合衆国アイオワ州ファイエットでDelos Horace Sullivanと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1920年にはアメリカ合衆国ミネソタ州ハバードバドウラ トウンシュイップに，1930年にはアメリカ合衆国ミネソタ州ハバードトッド トウンシュイップに住んでいた。彼女は1930年4月15日にアメリカ合衆国ミネソタ州ハバードネヴィスで58歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国ミネソタ州ハバードパーク ラピズグレエンウッド セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Carrie Moore(이)가 1871년 5월 30일 미국 위스콘신 주 크롤퍼드 프레이리 드 치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Cornelious S Moore(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mary Agnes Borland(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 1894년 7월 21일 미국 아이오와 주 파예트에서 Delos Horace Sullivan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 1920년도에 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드 바다라 타운시프에서, 1930년도에 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드 토드 타운시프에서 살았다. 그녀는 1930년 4월 15일 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드 네비스에서 58세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드 박 라피드즈 그린우드 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Carrie Moore nasceu em 30 de maio de 1871, em Prairie du Chien, Crawford, Wisconsin, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Cornelious S Moore, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Mary Agnes Borland, tinha 34 anos. Ela casou-se com Delos Horace Sullivan em 21 de julho de 1894, em Fayette, Iowa, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela viveu em Badoura Township, Hubbard, Minesota, Estados Unidos da América em 1920 e em Todd Township, Hubbard, Minesota, Estados Unidos da América em 1930. Ela faleceu em 15 de abril de 1930, em Nevis, Hubbard, Minesota, Estados Unidos da América, com 58 anos, e foi sepultada em Greenwood Cemetery, Park Rapids, Hubbard, Minesota, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Carrie Moore родилась 30 мая 1871 г. в местности преири ду чиен, кравфорд, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты, её отцу по имени Cornelious S Moore было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Mary Agnes Borland, 34. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Delos Horace Sullivan 21 июля 1894 г. в местности феиетт, Айова, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 3 дочери. Она жила в местности бадоура тоуншип, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты в 1920 году и в местности тодд тоуншип, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты в 1930 году. Она умерла 15 апреля 1930 г. в местности невис, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 58 лет и была похоронена в местности гринвуд кеметири, парк рапидс, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты.
